key_themes: Aggressive share buybacks and capital returns
Challenging 2025 results due to construction macro headwinds
H2 2025 recovery, solid 2026 start, and stable outlook
Attractive valuation with analyst upside to ~258p target
Disciplined M&A, strong balance sheet focus
summary: Somero Enterprises (SOM.L), a manufacturer of advanced concrete placing and leveling equipment, reported weaker 2025 results with revenue declining to ~$89m from $109m prior year amid macro uncertainty in construction markets, leading to lower profits, a reduced dividend, and a slight EPS miss. Management highlighted H2 improvement, healthy backlogs, a strong product pipeline, and a solid start to 2026, maintaining guidance for comparable performance this year. Sentiment is supported by proactive capital returns via an expanded $6m share buyback (up from $4m), ongoing share cancellations reducing the count to ~54m, and the stock's reasonable 14x PE with analyst targets implying 30-40% upside, though technicals remain mixed and the Smart Score signals underperform.
sources: 15+ web news articles (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, Investing.com, Reuters, company RNS), ~10 relevant X posts (mostly buyback/RNS focused), Investor discussion boards and earnings transcripts
